text: The surgeon performs a four-centimeter peri-umbilical incision and inserts a wound protector for specimen extraction and anastomosis. The small bowel is irrigated with sterile saline using a bladder syringe, and the ultrasound probe is prepared with a sterile cover for intraoperative assessment.

The ultrasound image shows thickening of the mucosa and submucosa in the terminal ileum and ileocecal region, with visible skip lesions. The top of the image displays normal small bowel, while the bottom shows inflamed, thickened bowel. The surgical field includes a limited ileocecal resection and side-to-side ileocolic anastomosis, along with three Heineken-Mikulitz strictureplasties.
